paragraphs.body: On a cold Friday afternoon, I asked my friend to go gun-shooting as I have never been. He started telling me that he has a better shot that me. I saw the challenge and took it, even though I had never shot a weapon before. The moment I shot that first bullet will forever be enshrined in my memory. I started off with something small, a pistol. The weight of the pistol really caught me off-guard, I thought to myself how did something so small, weigh so much. Standing there at the range, listening to nothing but my heartbeat and the wind, I fired a bullet. After several shots and a sore wrist, I finished the magazine. It disturbed the silence with an eerie bang. But it gave me a good feeling for what I signed up for. However, the shotgun gave me the opposite feeling. The ancient, rusty shotgun was handed to me by the old instructor. He knew what he was talking about because he made shooting the shotgun look simple. I still feel the recoil from the shot, it travelled through my body like it was trying to disconnect my shoulder from my body. The second shot was not any easier, this time nearly being swept off my feet like I was blown away by a tornado. I started to search for an alternative to prove to myself that I can take a challenge. There was something left but it was said to be the hardest weapon to operate. The sniper rifle. The metal of the trigger on the sniper is cold to the touch of your fingertips. The scent of the smoky gunpowder fills your nostrils as you get close to the scope. The hard rubber of the stock squeezes on your cheeks as you try to find the right zoom on the scope. Looking at the distant target through the zoom as I try to adjust the scope due to the wind. 50 to 100 yards doesn’t seem far on a football field but when you are aiming at a target, it feels like miles away. The slightest movement to the sniper can affect the whole shot. The pressure of the trigger is very heavy, all for a split second of a loud bang. The ricochet of the sniper sends a shockwave through your shoulder and your whole body. The sound of the gun echoes through the neighbouring woodland, the sound waves bouncing off every tree. The thought of how a small bullet can travel so fast is very interesting to me. The clink of the bullet flying out the sniper sounds like a church bell ringing. The sound of silence feels loud and daunting. I hear the echoes of my heart beating whilst I squeeze the trigger.

annotations.text: Standing there at the range, listening to nothing but my heartbeat and the wind, I fired a bullet. After several shots and a sore wrist, I finished the magazine. It disturbed the silence with an eerie bang.
annotations.context_right: But it gave me a go

annotations.text: I hear the echoes of my heart beating whilst I squeeze the trigger.

annotations.comment: great move to "zoom in" on the sniper rifle with a descriptive paragraph. This rhetorical move creates a sense of importance and urgency for your reader as we realize the rifle is going to  be important :)

paragraphs.body: A soldier is hopeful. A soldier is not selfish and puts his soul on the line for his cause. A soldier is not just a soldier. A soldier is a family person. A soldier is a father. A soldier is a mother. A soldier is a husband. A soldier is a wife. A soldier is a brother. A soldier is a sister. A soldier is not disloyal. A soldier would go through extreme lengths for there values. A soldier is fearful, not for their own life but for the life of their loved ones. A soldier remains focused under immense pressure. A soldier must be resilient and persevere with all the challenges they will be tasked with. A soldier may suffer injuries that change his life significantly after war, leading him to a new lifestyle. One that is filled with pain, drug-use and trauma. A soldier becomes emotionless as they see all of their comrades dying. A soldier becomes desensitised with all the life in the trenches. A soldier may have to remain on the cold, lonely battlefield for his whole life. A soldier becomes weak as they eat the nutrition-less rations and being around all sorts of sicknesses and diseases. A soldiers journey back home is never promised. The time of the soldiers return home is uncertain. The work a soldier does may never be commended. But through all of this a soldier must have hope.

annotations.text: soldier is a family person. A soldier is a father. A soldier is a mother. A soldier is a husband. A soldier is a wife. A soldier is a brother. A soldier is a sister.
annotations.comment: Who is a soldier to you, personally? Do you have members of your family in the service? Or who've had to fight? Let's talk about how you might leverage this definition paragraph for the purposes of your essay.

paragraphs.body: Hope(v)- ‘have the theological virtue of hope; trust in (God’s word); to have trust, have confidence; assume confidently or trust, a word of unknown origin’

Hope(n)- ‘Confidence in the future , expectation of something desired’ The history of the word ‘hope’ has not really evolved over time. However, the way the word is being used has changed. Coming from the 1200s, ‘hope’ was used as a noun. I have a lot of hope in our future is an example of how the word could be used. Later in the 1300s, the word hope could be used as a verb. I hope that you have a great time at the party. I hope I get the promotion at work. The verb for hope can be used for self-hope and also hope for other people. The word hope carries plenty of positive connotations. It carries the belief in the possibility of a better future. It can help lighten the load for people who are anxious or nervous about what the future holds for them. A negative connotation of hope can be that it can show unrealistic expectations and then allow a person to doubt more than ever. I relate to the idea that the word hope carries the belief in the possibility of a better future. When I started playing football, my journey for the near future was not clear at all. I took a huge risk by starting to play football at such a late age. I felt like I was aiming at a target blindfolded. All I had was my size, the belief of my family and friends and hope. Hope that I will succeed even when others doubt me. Hope that I will challenge myself and win. Hope that I can provide a better future for my family. Hope that all the sacrifices that my parents have made would not go out in vain. Hope that all my brothers and sisters in Sudan will stay safe in this tough and daunting time in our nation's history. I have been very fortunate to have live in the United Kingdom my whole life, far from any struggles in Sudan. My father decided to move there with my mother after their marriage. My 3 siblings and I have been blessed with many opportunities just because of this one decision. Our family vacations were always to Sudan and my mum always used to bring back clothes for her nieces and nephews. Sometimes I sit and think about what my life could have been if I was raised in Sudan. Especially with the civil war going on, there have been plenty of deaths and people have fled the country. A country that would never sleep now has streets so quiet you can hear your heartbeat. How would I have felt to wake up and be the target that people are aiming at?

annotations.text: felt like I was aiming at a target blindfolded. All I had was my size, the belief of my family and friends and hope.
